title: Technical documentation snippet
description: Tech writing — precise, structured, with technical terms
text: The API returns a JSON response with three fields: status, data, and error. If status equals "ok", the data field contains the requested resource. Otherwise, error provides a human-readable message and an error code.

tips.text: Technical writing leans on the same handful of terms over and over. Once your fingers learn them, the page moves quickly.
A colon announces what comes next: a list, a definition, a result. Get comfortable with it and structured prose holds no fear.

tips.target: The API returns a JSON response with three fields: status, data, and error. If status equals "ok",

tips.text: Read API and JSON as single units, the way you would read a name. Letter-by-letter is where the stumbles creep in.

tips.target: the data field contains the requested resource. Otherwise, error provides a human-readable message and an error code.

character_tips.anna: Technical pages turn up in nearly every job now. Learn their rhythm and you are ready for the work. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: People who build things write far more than they expect to. Clean technical prose is a skill that pays. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: The terms look strange at first, then they become old friends. Type them whole and keep moving. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Clear, exact, no clutter. Good documentation reads like a recipe, and so should your typing of it. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
